content: The spelling variant `reflexion` (the older British English variant of the modern standard `reflection`) can act as a linguistic mirror for LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where different orthographic representations of the same core concept parallel multiple inconsistent solving perspectives that boost LLM reflective performance. This framing suggests that intentionally exposing LLMs to valid variant representations of core problems during self-reflection can improve their ability to critique and revise their own outputs.
